nodes.label: AI as research tool for historical details
nodes.grounding_span: I ask a lot of ultra-specific questions about the time period that would take a long time to find out via a Google search alone

nodes.label: AI as creative writing substitute
nodes.label_negative: Human-authored creative writing with distinct voice
nodes.grounding_span: I never ask AI to write passages for me

nodes.label: AI truthfulness and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: it will lie and offer something false as the answer

nodes.label: AI agreeableness and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: I find it too agreeable, accepting false premises too easily

nodes.label: Social acceptability of AI use among authors
nodes.label_negative: Stigma and boycott risk for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: using AI is very looked down upon

nodes.label: Authorial voice and creative authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: the most important part of being an established author is having a distinct voice

nodes.label: Efficient research and factual accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: it would just take a lot longer

nodes.label: Professional reputation and social acceptance
nodes.grounding_span: readers will boycott an author if they think they have used AI

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for research efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I ask a lot of ultra-specific questions about the time period that would take a long time to find out

nodes.label: Firm rejection of AI for creative writing
nodes.grounding_span: I never ask AI to write passages for me

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination and sycophancy
nodes.grounding_span: it will lie and offer something false as the answer

nodes.label: Concealment of AI use due to stigma
nodes.grounding_span: I would rather just not mention it at all

nodes.label: Question-driven discrete interactions
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to ask specific questions and leave it at that

nodes.label: Clear boundary setting between research and creation
nodes.grounding_span: I will never ask an AI simply to write on my behalf
